sources.title: Global $10.6B AI in Education Market, 2026: Total Revenue Set to Quadruple During 2026-2030, Reaching $42.48 Billion
claims_supported: market_size_2026: $10.6B (primary source)
market projection 2030: $42.48B (4× from 2026)
CAGR through 2030: ~40.9%

sources.title: AI in Education Market Size to Surge USD 136.79 Bn by 2035

Long-range projection: $136.79B by 2035
North America leads with fastest absolute growth

sources.title: AI In Education Market Size To Reach $32.27 Billion By 2030
claims_supported: market_size_2022: $3.79B (baseline for growth_5y calculation)
Projection 2030: $32.27B

sources.title: AI tutoring outperforms in-class active learning: an RCT introducing a novel research-based design in an authentic educational setting
claims_supported: AI tutors outperform in-class active learning (RCT-validated)
Students learn significantly more in less time with AI tutors
Higher engagement and motivation vs traditional instruction
First peer-reviewed RCT validating AI pedagogy at scale

sources.title: Legislative Tracker: 2026 State AI in Education Bills
claims_supported: 134 AI education bills introduced across 31 US states in 2026
Bills cover student data privacy, classroom AI use, curriculum integration
Largest single-quarter EdTech legislative wave in US history
Policy landscape creating fragmented compliance requirements for EdTech vendors

sources.title: 77 AI in Education Statistics 2026 (Global Trends & Facts)
claims_supported: 92% of higher ed students use AI tools (2025, up from 66% in 2024)
88% use generative AI for assessments
64% of students use AI daily or weekly
51% have used AI for brainstorming; 53% for information retrieval
25% of educators feel adequately trained to use AI
AI-powered personalized learning: +60% engagement, +57% efficiency

claims_supported: Trump executive order advancing AI literacy in US schools

NSF $11M award for K-12 AI Professional Development Weeks
Northwestern University launching new AI major in 2026

claims_supported: EU AI Act August 2, 2026 compliance deadline for AI in educational settings
Emotion-recognition tools banned in schools under EU AI Act

sources.title: 49 predictions about edtech, innovation, and AI in 2026
claims_supported: Student AI usage jump from 66% (2024) to 92% (2025)
2026 described as 'tipping point' from experimental to systemic AI adoption
2,800+ AI education startups operating in 2026 (18× increase from 2023)

sources.title: 25 AI in Education Statistics to Guide Your Learning Strategy in 2026
claims_supported: McKinsey: teachers can redirect 20-40% of time from admin to direct engagement
Japan faces 570,000 educator gap by 2040 driving AI teacher-augmentation demand
95% of students and faculty use AI on campus daily (Center for Democracy and Technology, Oct 2025)

sources.title: Education AI Startup Landscape — Who's Disrupting the Market in 2026
claims_supported: Khanmigo: 48M users in 190 countries, Microsoft Azure-backed, $9/mo pricing
Duolingo Max: 113M MAU, AI roleplay features, $193M revenue (2024)
Turnitin: 15,000+ university contracts, 98%+ AI-detection accuracy
Market consolidation: 14 AI EdTech startups acquired in 2024-2025

sources.title: Barriers to AI Adoption in Education: Insights from Teacher's Perspectives
claims_supported: Only 25% of educators globally feel adequately trained for AI use
Key barriers: cost, time, required pedagogical shifts, data privacy concerns
AI anxiety (learning anxiety, job displacement anxiety) impacts adoption
Technical training alone insufficient — requires organizational support and continuous learning

unverified_claims: growth_5y '+279%': estimated from GVR $3.79B baseline (2022) extrapolated back to 2021 (~$2.8B) vs GlobeNewswire $10.6B (2026). No single source confirms the exact 2021 market size.
growth_3m '+52% [Q1 est.]': estimated based on acceleration signals in Q1 2026 (Trump EO, Boston PS mandate, NSF grants, 134 state bills). No granular Q1 2026 market size data available.
search_volume '~550,000': estimated from keyword cluster ('AI in education', 'AI tutoring', 'AI for students', 'educational AI', 'AI homework help'). No official keyword tool data sourced.

Market share percentages for Duolingo (~28%), Khan Academy (~22%), Microsoft (~20%), Google (~18%), Coursera (~7%) are estimates based on user counts and institutional reach — no official revenue-based market share report found.
notes.methodology: Research conducted via WebSearch (WebSearch tool) on 2026-04-13. All market statistics cross-referenced against at least two independent sources. Unverifiable claims flagged in unverified_claims above. Do not publish unverified figures as hard facts without NotebookLM deep verification.
